story_summary: A registered user can securely reset their forgotten password by receiving and using a password-reset link sent via email to regain account access.

test_cases.title: Successful password reset with valid email and new password
test_cases.description: A registered user initiates a password reset using their registered email, receives a secure reset link, sets a valid new password, and successfully logs in with the updated credentials.
preconditions: User is registered in the system with a confirmed email address
user currently cannot access account due to forgotten password.

steps.action: Navigate to the 'Forgot Password' page and enter the registered email address.
steps.expected_result: System accepts the email input and triggers sending a password reset email.

steps.action: Check the registered email inbox, locate the password reset email, and click the unique reset link.
steps.expected_result: Reset link opens a 'Set New Password' page prompting for a new password.

steps.action: Enter a valid new password that meets system requirements and submit the form.
steps.expected_result: System accepts the new password, updates it in the account, and displays a confirmation message.

steps.action: Attempt to log in with the registered email and the newly set password.
steps.expected_result: Login is successful and user gains access to their account.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Password reset is completed successfully; user can access the account using the new password; the reset link is invalidated after use.

test_cases.title: Password reset and login with password containing special characters
test_cases.description: A registered user resets their password using a valid reset link and chooses a new password that includes special characters, then logs in successfully.
preconditions: User is registered and has access to their registered email
password complexity includes support for special characters.

steps.action: Request a password reset by submitting the registered email address on the reset page.
steps.expected_result: System sends a password reset email with a secure link to the user's email.

steps.action: Open the password reset email and follow the provided reset link.
steps.expected_result: A secure password reset form is displayed.

steps.action: Enter a new password containing letters, numbers, and special characters (e.g., P@ssw0rd!) and submit.
steps.expected_result: System validates and updates the password successfully, showing a confirmation message.

steps.action: Log in using the registered email and the new password with special characters.
steps.expected_result: User logs in successfully and accesses their account.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Password reset flows correctly with passwords containing special characters; user authentication is successful with the new credentials.

test_cases.title: Password reset flow using username as identifier
test_cases.description: User initiates password reset using their registered username instead of email, receives reset link via registered email, resets password, and logs in successfully.
preconditions: User is registered with a username and an associated confirmed email address

steps.action: Navigate to the password reset page and enter the registered username as an identifier.
steps.expected_result: System validates the username and sends a reset email to the registered email address.

steps.action: Open the received email and click the password reset link.
steps.expected_result: User is taken to the password reset page.

steps.action: Input a new valid password and submit the reset form.
steps.expected_result: Password is updated successfully and confirmation message is shown.

steps.action: Use the username and new password to log into the system.
steps.expected_result: Login is successful, and user gains access to the account.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Password reset via username identifier works properly; user can log in with the new password after reset.

test_cases.title: Submit empty user identifier to initiate password reset
test_cases.description: Test the system's behavior when a registered user attempts to initiate a password reset with an empty identifier field (no username or email).
preconditions: User is on the password reset request page.

steps.action: Leave the user identifier input field empty.
steps.expected_result: System rejects the request with an appropriate validation error indicating that the identifier cannot be empty.

steps.action: Submit the password reset request.
steps.expected_result: No password reset email is sent.

test_cases.expected_outcome: System rejects the password reset request when the user identifier is empty and no email is sent.

test_cases.title: Use expired password reset link
test_cases.description: Verify system response when a user attempts to reset their password using a reset link that has expired after its allowed validity period.
preconditions: User has previously received a password reset email but the link validity period has passed.

steps.action: Open the expired password reset email link.
steps.expected_result: System denies the password reset attempt and informs the user that the reset link has expired.

steps.action: Attempt to enter a new password and submit.
steps.expected_result: User is prompted to initiate a new password reset request.

test_cases.expected_outcome: User cannot reset password with an expired link; system informs user of expiration and prompts for new reset request.

test_cases.title: Submit new password at maximum length supported
test_cases.description: Test how the system handles resetting password when the new password is at the maximum allowed character length (e.g., 256 characters).
preconditions: User has received a valid password reset link.

steps.action: Open the valid reset link.
steps.expected_result: Password reset page is loaded.

steps.action: Enter a new password consisting of the maximum allowed number of characters supported by the system (e.g., 256).
steps.expected_result: System accepts the new password if it meets all other requirements.

steps.action: Submit the new password.
steps.expected_result: Password is successfully updated; user receives confirmation and can login with the new password.

test_cases.expected_outcome: System handles maximum length passwords correctly; password is updated and user notified.

test_cases.title: Simultaneous multiple password reset requests from the same user
test_cases.description: Check system handling and potential race conditions when the same user initiates multiple password reset requests concurrently.
preconditions: User is registered with an email address and can trigger reset emails.

steps.action: Simultaneously submit multiple password reset requests for the same user identifier (e.g., through parallel browser sessions or API calls).
steps.expected_result: System limits the number of valid password reset tokens to one or a defined maximum.

steps.action: Verify the number and validity of reset emails sent.
steps.expected_result: Only one token remains valid for resetting the password; others are either invalidated or expire. User can successfully reset the password using only the most recent valid link. System prevents abuse or spamming of reset emails.

test_cases.expected_outcome: System manages concurrent reset requests by limiting valid tokens and prevents abuse.

test_cases.title: Password reset attempt with invalid format token in URL
test_cases.description: Validate system response when a password reset link contains a token with an invalid format or tampered string.
preconditions: User has an invalid or manipulated reset link URL.

steps.action: Open a password reset link with a token containing invalid characters or a truncated token.
steps.expected_result: System detects the invalid token format and rejects the request.

steps.action: Attempt to enter a new password and submit.
steps.expected_result: User is informed that the reset link is invalid or corrupted and prompted to request a new password reset link.

test_cases.expected_outcome: System rejects password reset attempts with invalid token formats and notifies user.

test_cases.title: Test SQL Injection in Password Reset Identifier Input
test_cases.description: Verify that the password reset initiation form properly sanitizes and handles input to prevent SQL Injection attacks through the user identifier (email or username).
preconditions: The password reset page/form is accessible.

steps.action: Navigate to the password reset initiation page.
steps.expected_result: Page loads successfully.

steps.action: In the user identifier input field, enter the payload: "' OR '1'='1"
steps.expected_result: Input field accepts the string.

steps.action: Submit the form to trigger the password reset process.
steps.expected_result: The system must not execute unintended SQL commands.

steps.action: Observe the server response and system behavior.
steps.expected_result: The application should reject the input with an error or send a generic password reset email only if the user exists. No database errors or abnormal behavior should be exposed. No account enumeration should be possible via error messages.

test_cases.expected_outcome: System prevents SQL injection attacks on password reset inputs and avoids account enumeration.

test_cases.title: Test Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via Password Reset Link
test_cases.description: Verify that the password reset token and all parameters in the password reset link are properly validated and encoded to prevent reflected or stored XSS attacks.
preconditions: A valid password reset link exists or can be intercepted.

steps.action: Capture a valid password reset URL sent via email (e.g., https://example.com/reset?token=VALIDTOKEN).
steps.expected_result: Valid URL is available.

steps.action: Modify the token parameter to include an XSS payload, for example: <script>alert('XSS')</script>
steps.expected_result: URL includes XSS payload.

steps.action: Access the manipulated URL in a browser.
steps.expected_result: Page attempts to load.

steps.action: Observe if the script executes or if the input is safely handled.
steps.expected_result: The application must not execute any injected scripts. The reset page should sanitize or encode the token parameter to neutralize scripts. An error message or invalid token message should be shown if the token is malformed.

test_cases.expected_outcome: System prevents XSS attacks via password reset token handling.

test_cases.title: Test CSRF Protection on Password Reset Submission
test_cases.description: Verify that the form submission for setting a new password via the reset link includes CSRF protection to prevent unauthorized password changes via cross-site request forgery.
preconditions: A valid password reset link is obtained.

steps.action: Open the password reset form with a valid token in a browser.
steps.expected_result: Form loads.

steps.action: Inspect the form elements for the presence of a CSRF token (e.g., hidden input field with unpredictable value).
steps.expected_result: CSRF token is present in the form.

steps.action: Attempt to submit the form by replicating the POST request without including the CSRF token.
steps.expected_result: System rejects the request.

steps.action: Optionally, attempt to submit a crafted form from a different domain/browser session lacking a valid CSRF token.
steps.expected_result: System rejects the request.

test_cases.expected_outcome: System enforces CSRF protection on password reset submissions.

test_cases.title: Test Rate Limiting on Password Reset Requests
test_cases.description: Verify the system protects against brute force or enumeration attacks by enforcing rate limiting on the number of password reset requests per user or IP address.
preconditions: The password reset initiation endpoint is available.

steps.action: Send a high volume of password reset requests for the same user identifier or email (e.g., 20 requests within 1 minute) using automated tools or scripts.
steps.expected_result: System accepts requests up to limit.

steps.action: Observe the responses from the server for each request.
steps.expected_result: Excess requests are rejected with appropriate error messages without revealing user existence.

steps.action: Send password reset requests for multiple different identifiers from the same IP to check IP-based limits.
steps.expected_result: System applies IP-based rate limiting.

steps.action: Check for any account lockout or temporary blocking feedback messages.
steps.expected_result: System may lockout or temporarily block abusive requesters.

test_cases.expected_outcome: System enforces rate limiting to prevent abuse and enumeration via password reset requests.

test_cases.title: Test Handling of Oversized Payloads in New Password Submission
test_cases.description: Verify that the system properly validates and enforces maximum length limits on the new password field to prevent buffer overflow or denial of service attacks via oversized payloads.
preconditions: Access to password reset form with a valid token.

steps.action: Enter a new password containing an oversized payload, for example, a string of 10,000 'A' characters.
steps.expected_result: Input accepts large payload.

steps.action: Submit the password reset form with the large password.
steps.expected_result: System rejects the input with a clear error message indicating password length limits.

steps.action: Observe the system response and behavior.
steps.expected_result: No crashes, timeouts, or unexpected errors occur.

test_cases.expected_outcome: System enforces maximum password length limits and handles large inputs safely.
